task_completion.description: Load the countries vector layer into the GIS software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The layers panel or map view shows the 'ne_110m_admin_0_countries' shapefile loaded.

task_completion.description: Load the global Natural Earth physical raster into the GIS software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The layers panel or map view shows the 'NE1_HR_LC_SR_W_DR' raster loaded. (Note: The agent may have had to manually download this if it was missing).

task_completion.description: Select or isolate the boundary of Bolivia from the countries vector layer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Bolivia is highlighted (usually in a distinct selection color like yellow) on the map, or a query/filter dialog is visible isolating 'Bolivia'.

task_completion.description: Open and configure the geoprocessing tool to clip the raster using the vector boundary.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box for a clipping tool (e.g., 'Clip Raster with Polygon', 'Extract by Mask', or 'Crop') is visible, with the input raster and the Bolivia vector layer (or selection) set as the mask/clipping extent.

task_completion.description: Export the resulting clipped raster to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As', 'Export', or tool output dialog shows the exact path '~/gvsig_data/exports/bolivia_relief.tif' being used to save the final clipped raster.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIS graphical user interface (e.g., gvSIG or QGIS) to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a desktop GIS application rather than exclusively typing GDAL/Python commands in a terminal.

integrity.description: The agent actually performed a clip/extract operation rather than just cropping a rectangle manually.
integrity.visual_evidence: The tool used is a mask/polygon extraction tool, and the resulting raster preview (if visible) follows the irregular topological boundary of Bolivia, not just a bounding box.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the task by simply creating an empty file or downloading a pre-made solution.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file 'bolivia_relief.tif' is generated as a direct result of the GIS software's export/geoprocessing execution, not via a 'touch' command or a direct web download of the final answer.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is ~/gvsig_data/exports/bolivia_relief.tif. A correctly clipped raster of Bolivia from the 10m Natural Earth dataset should have dimensions of approximately 727x788 pixels and contain valid physical/elevation data (not a blank image). The agent must use the vector layer at ~/gvsig_data/countries/ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp as the clipping mask.
